missions.statement: AOF quality means repeatedly choosing the right problem, assigning the right actors, making runtime-backed decisions, exposing state to humans, governing release truth, and learning from outcomes without replacing evidence with activity volume.

stakeholders.name: Operator or project owner
needs: Understand what AOF is doing, why it is doing it, and what should happen next.
harmsToAvoid: AI appears busy while working on the wrong problem or hiding weak evidence.

stakeholders.name: Managed-project user
needs: Bring AOF into a project safely with clear topology, validation, and release boundaries.
harmsToAvoid: AOF automation bypasses project governance or writes unsafe changes into product main.

stakeholders.name: AI actor or Codex worker
needs: Receive scoped, skillful, evidence-backed assignments with resources, policies, and review criteria.
harmsToAvoid: Generic roleplay without capability fit, resource context, or policy constraints.

stakeholders.name: Governance owner
needs: Review decisions, release claims, and quality verdicts through traceable artifacts.
harmsToAvoid: Release or quality claims drift from runtime artifacts.

contexts.scope: AOF v5.0.0 self-hosting and managed-project runtime quality baseline.
constraints: Do not treat activity counts as quality.
Do not treat number of agents, roles, artifacts, tests, releases, or documentation volume as quality itself.
Do not treat dashboard existence or viewer visual appeal as quality itself.
Do not convert raw needs directly into projects.
Do not claim runtime-backed quality without command or artifact evidence.
Do not claim actor skillfulness without skill, capability, resource, policy, and review evidence.
Do not call an iteration a loop unless it has goal, execution, verification, and stop condition.
Do not automate a flow before a manual run has proven its artifact chain, review path, and stop boundary.
assumptions: AOF v5.0.0 release artifacts are current.
QIF v0.2.1 package validation proves structural integrity but not semantic truth.
Human governance remains required for high-impact quality claims.

risks.statement: AOF may execute well on a need that should have been reframed, rejected, deferred, or tested first.
risks.lossBoundary: No project should be created from a raw need without Need Validation or an explicit experiment requirement.

risks.statement: The orchestrator may answer, review, or self-review without actually using the AOF runtime.
risks.lossBoundary: Direction, review, self-review, and retrospective answers must include runtime command or artifact references.

risks.statement: AOF may assign a generic actor without enough skill, capability, resource, policy, or review evidence.
risks.lossBoundary: Governed actor execution must be backed by actor skill packet, assignment evaluation, and execution gate evidence.

risks.statement: AOF runtime state may be technically present but not recognizable to the human operator.
risks.lossBoundary: The operator must be able to identify current mission, release, frontier, blockers, next action, and runtime-backed status without reading raw JSON.

risks.statement: AOF release claims may drift from package metadata, active release manifest, docs, tasks, or GitHub tags.
risks.lossBoundary: Release-state audit must pass before a release is considered closed.

risks.statement: AOF quality may be confused with counts of agents, roles, artifacts, tests, releases, documentation, dashboards, viewers, or reviews.
risks.lossBoundary: Activity-count and presentation indicators must be evidence-only and linked to a Quality Intent, risk, and loss boundary.

risks.statement: AOF may treat repeated AI output as a loop even when no independent verification, stop condition, or accepted-change evidence exists.
risks.lossBoundary: AOF loop claims must include goal, execution, verification, stop condition, and budget or human-review boundary.

knowledgeSources.title: AOF core model
knowledgeSources.extractionMethod: quality intent extraction from core boundaries

knowledgeSources.title: AOF v5.0 release definition and checklist
AOF runtime active release and situation assessment artifacts
knowledgeSources.extractionMethod: runtime command output and artifact review

qualityIntents.statement: AOF must validate or reframe an incoming need before creating a project.
qualityIntents.purpose: Prevent high-quality execution on the wrong problem.
acceptanceCriteria: Raw needs are not directly converted into project work.
Need Validation can reject, defer, request evidence, or require an experiment.
Project Charter is based on a Validated Need.

qualityIntents.statement: AOF direction, review, self-review, and retrospective answers must be runtime-backed.
qualityIntents.purpose: Prevent orchestrator answers from drifting into ungrounded analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one runtime command is executed for direction, review, self-review, or retrospective claims.
The answer includes command or artifact references.
Stale or missing runtime state weakens the answer instead of being hidden.
Loop evidence includes goal, execution, verification, and stop condition.

qualityIntents.statement: AOF actor assignment must be skillful rather than generic.
qualityIntents.purpose: Ensure actors work from explicit skill, capability, resource, policy, output, and review evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Actor skill packet exists before governed actor execution.
Capability fit is explicit.
Maker and Checker responsibilities are separated in evidence.
Resource and policy gates are evaluated.
Weak or missing skill evidence can block, degrade, or request evidence.

qualityIntents.statement: AOF runtime state must be recognizable to a human operator.
qualityIntents.purpose: Make organizational state understandable without raw JSON reconstruction.
acceptanceCriteria: Current mission, release, frontier, next action, blockers, and runtime-backed status are visible.
Human Recognition Interface does not become a second source of truth.
Visible actor state is derived from canonical runtime artifacts.

qualityIntents.statement: AOF release state must stay truthful and auditable.
qualityIntents.purpose: Prevent release claims from drifting from package, runtime, docs, task, and GitHub evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Package metadata, bootstrap version, active release manifest, release definition, checklist, and notes align.
Shipped-release tasks are not left open.
Release-state audit passes.
GitHub tag and release claims match local release artifacts.

qualityIntents.statement: AOF must preserve learning and governance loops.
qualityIntents.purpose: Keep post-release and low-confidence situations from turning into automatic implementation momentum.
acceptanceCriteria: Outcome, self-audit, alignment pulse, or review evidence can update the next value slice.
Low-confidence, conflicting, or context-mismatched results trigger governance review.
Next implementation work is not opened by inertia after release closure.
Loops define max iterations, token or cost budget, confidence threshold, human review threshold, or stop/defer/reopen rule.
Accepted-change economics can be used as evidence only when tied to a Quality Intent and loss boundary.

evidenceItems.finding: AOF core and README define Need Validation as a mandatory pre-project gate and allow reject, defer, request evidence, or require experiment outcomes.
situation-assess reports active release 5.0.0, no truth conflicts, no primary frontier task, and runtime-backed next action for direction review.
v5.0 release artifacts define actor skill packet, assignment evaluation, execution gate, negative benchmark, and HRI projection as release gates.
Current visibility artifacts include operator brief, mission control, runtime execution, timeline, tree position, and Skillful Actor HRI projection evidence.
v5.0.0 active release manifest, bootstrap version, governance release contract, release definition, checklist, notes, local tag, and GitHub release were aligned at release closure.

QIF requires quality intents, evidence, verdicts, and governance; AOF post-v5.0 state intentionally defers next implementation work to runtime-backed direction review and now treats loop claims as bounded goal/execution/verification/stop-condition claims.

evaluations.scope: AOF v5.0.0 baseline quality definition using QIF v0.2.1.

verdicts.rationale: structurally_achieved=yes; runtime_achieved=partial; operator_validated=not-yet; externally_validated=not-yet. Need Validation is explicit in AOF core boundaries and remains a project-creation gate.
verdicts.residualRisk: Semantic need quality still depends on real user evidence, human review, and operational feedback.

verdicts.rationale: structurally_achieved=yes; runtime_achieved=yes; operator_validated=partial; externally_validated=not-yet. Current runtime state is inspectable and direction claims are tied to runtime commands and artifacts.
verdicts.residualRisk: Future operators can still bypass runtime unless enforcement and visibility remain active.

verdicts.rationale: structurally_achieved=yes; runtime_achieved=yes; operator_validated=not-yet; externally_validated=not-yet. v5 release gates and artifacts make actor skillfulness explicit and falsifiable.
verdicts.residualRisk: Skill evidence quality will need real-world actor performance feedback and reproduction across projects.

verdicts.rationale: structurally_achieved=yes; runtime_achieved=yes; operator_validated=partial; externally_validated=not-yet. Operator-facing artifacts expose current state and Skillful Actor projection from canonical runtime artifacts.
verdicts.residualRisk: Visual comprehension and operator UX remain future Quality Intent candidates, such as QIN-AOF-HUMAN-UX or QIN-AOF-VISUAL-COMPREHENSION.

verdicts.rationale: structurally_achieved=yes; runtime_achieved=yes; operator_validated=partial; externally_validated=not-yet. Release state was aligned across package, bootstrap, active manifest, docs, task state, tag, and GitHub release.
verdicts.residualRisk: Future releases must rerun release-state audit and avoid open shipped-release tasks; current package does not prove market or semantic value.

verdicts.rationale: structurally_achieved=yes; runtime_achieved=partial; operator_validated=not-yet; externally_validated=not-yet. QIF governance concepts align with AOF's post-release direction-review boundary and bounded loop definition.
verdicts.residualRisk: Learning loop strength depends on future outcome evidence, reproduction tests, operational feedback, and governance decisions.

governanceEvents.reason: AOF must prevent non-runtime-backed orchestrator answers from being treated as quality decisions.
governanceEvents.decision: Approved as baseline AOF quality intent.

governanceEvents.reason: v5.0 makes actor skillfulness a first-class release quality boundary.
governanceEvents.decision: Approved as baseline AOF quality intent.

governanceEvents.reason: Human Recognition should remain part of the current baseline while visual comprehension and operator UX remain future Quality Intent candidates.
governanceEvents.decision: Approved as baseline intent with residual UX validation risk.

criteria.statement: Quality is defined by Quality Intents, risks, evidence, verdicts, and acceptance gates rather than activity volume.
Each Quality Intent links to stakeholder value and a loss boundary risk.
Verdicts are traceable to evidence and reproducible confidence inputs.
Current verdicts distinguish structural and runtime achievement from operator and external validation.
Validator pass is not treated as semantic truth.
Low-confidence, conflicting, or context-mismatched results require governance review instead of hidden acceptance.
Loop claims require goal, execution, verification, stop condition, and budget or human-review boundary.
Accepted-change economics are candidate evidence only, not standalone quality proof.
